mentions.note: Classic recession hedge now being sold; war reaction irrational; time-honored Wall Street proxy for economic deterioration.
Rallied 18% on strong quarter; embraces lower-income segment with ~85% of sales at $2 or below; smart assortments help cash-strapped consumers stretch budgets.
Blowout quarter with sensational numbers and bright forecast despite consumer facing elevated fuel costs and inflation headwinds.
Dollar store recession-trade model permanently broken. Weak consumers trading down away from stores, not up to them. Inflation and tariffs eliminated $1 item advantage; investment thesis inverted.
Value retailer; real products; on Cramer's list of understandable, in-demand companies.
Trade-down retail theme; more adventuresome investor play; better for consumers watching budgets.
Off game alongside DG but has track record of surprising upside; don't dismiss but stay cautious.
Plunged 4.2%; expected uptick as economy slows failed to materialize; suggests deep consumer distress.
